---
# Source Card: Diagnostic Value of a Protocolized In-Depth Evaluation of Pediatric Bone Marrow Failure

## Summary
This peer-reviewed multi-center prospective cohort (50 Dutch pediatric BMF patients, 2017-2021; Frontiers in Immunology, 2022) establishes a protocolized diagnostic pathway for suspected pediatric bone marrow failure. For a pediatric CDS module it supplies concrete enrollment cutoffs for cytopenia (neutrophil < 1.5 x10^9/L, platelet < 150 x10^9/L, or reticulocytopenia), a bioinformatic variant-filter threshold (gnomAD allele frequency > 5% excluded), a defined 172-gene BMF panel, and a workflow ordering (exclude malignant/transient causes first, then genome-wide WES+CNV/SNP-array plus functional tests for all). It reports a 40% causative-diagnosis yield and recommends full genetic analysis in every suspected BMF child. All numeric points are study inclusion/analysis criteria, not a population reference-interval study — the CDS converter must treat them as such.

- (ev_002) Thrombocytopenia enrollment cutoff < 150 x10^9/L — locator: Methods > Patients — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:812271628f9d81d1a4ee65ef79dd0bc8f0fdf23e1b4373c379b2db01878e364c]"
- (ev_003) Variant-interpretation filter: gnomAD allele frequency > 5% excluded — locator: Methods > WES — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ae419a907fa9b618980501a61ede917cce198265cd685332772eca88340d81a2]"

- (ev_005) First step = exclude malignant/transient causes; refer to pediatric oncology if leukemia suspected — locator: Methods / Figure 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:6c24c1e57b9e2a6aca9930eba5517408dd63c39bd3689f3398798f560c9073ee]"

- (ev_007) NK-cell % lower in SAA vs constitutional (3.0 vs 9.0; P<0.05) — locator: Results > Immunological Screening — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:00e83396acd97b2d6ade2e0c7534549fcd794c620fd1486ccb8f34609902cb45]"
- (ev_008) BMF gene panel = 172 genes + HPO terms — locator: Methods > WES — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e4f11e016b253fab30e8659fb0b4cdb342311adf2617ff26d6e65652f02d4ae1]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric patients aged 0-18 years (median 8 y; ≤2 y = 16%, 3-11 y = 56%, ≥12 y = 28%), 29 male / 21 female, four Dutch academic centers, 2017-2021. Neonatal/gestational-age stratification is NOT provided.
- Assay/method dependence: cytopenia cutoffs from automated full blood count (analyzer not specified). Genetic thresholds are pipeline-dependent: Agilent SureSelect Human All Exon V7 + Illumina NovaSeq 6000, MAGPIE (BWA-MEM/GATK), Variant Effect Predictor, gnomAD, ACMG classification; CNV via NxClinical; SNP array via CytoScanHD (Affymetrix); telomere length via Flow-FISH; FA screen via mitomycin-C chromosome breakage. NK-cell % via lymphocyte immunophenotyping (flow cytometry).
- Lifecycle: cutoffs reflect the 2017-2021 study protocol / 2022 publication; no formal effective/retire dates or guideline version numbers are stated. gnomAD/ACMG referenced as the classification frameworks.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes — scope boundary and referral trigger: the protocol's first step screens for leukemia/malignant marrow disease and refers suspected cases to a pediatric oncology center BEFORE the BMF genetic/functional workup (ev_005). Transient/reversible and secondary causes (viral infection, malnutrition) are excluded first. A CDS "suspected BMF" pathway must gate on prior exclusion of malignancy and transient causes; this is a proposal for routing, not a diagnostic or treatment directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- The cytopenia cutoffs (ANC < 1.5 x10^9/L; platelet < 150 x10^9/L) are STUDY ENROLLMENT criteria for "suspected BMF," not age-partitioned reference intervals; do not repurpose them as normal/abnormal reference limits without a reference-interval source.
- No age/sex/gestational-age-partitioned reference values are provided; neonatal thresholds are NOT locatable in this source — record as a GAP for any neonatal CDS use.
- A quantitative NK-cell decision cutoff is NOT locatable — only group medians (3.0 vs 9.0) with a P-value; treat as descriptive, not a rule threshold (GAP: NK-cell abnormal-cutoff not defined in this source).
- No SAA severity-grade numeric cutoffs (moderate/severe/very severe) are given in-text — the source cites WHO definitions by reference only (GAP: SAA severity thresholds not locatable in this source).
- Single-country reference-center cohort, n=50, 60% remained without a genetic cause — yield figures may not generalize.
- No direct conflict with other cited sources recorded here. Any conflict with formal pediatric CBC reference-interval sources (e.g., CALIPER) must be preserved as a conflict, not merged, since this source's cutoffs are enrollment criteria of a different intent.
